message:
fix(auth): recursive third-party discharge verification with cycle detection

MacaroonToken::verify_with_discharges now performs full recursive
verification of third-party discharge tokens instead of only checking
presence and binding signature. Previously, nested discharges (a
discharge that itself has third-party caveats requiring further
discharges) were presence-checked but their caveat chains were not
walked, meaning a discharge with failing first-party caveats would
still pass verification.

Key changes:
- verify_with_discharges_inner: new recursive entry point that tracks
  active discharges on a stack (Vec<usize> using pointer-derived IDs)
  for cycle detection. Cycles immediately fail with discharge_invalid.
- verify_discharge_signature: extracted signature verification that
  handles both root tokens (plain HMAC comparison) and bound discharge
  tokens (HMAC(binding_signature, unbound_signature) comparison).
- verify_caveat_chain: walks the caveat list, calling
  advance_first_party_caveat for first-party caveats and recursing
  into verify_with_discharges_inner for third-party caveats with the
  recovered caveat key as the discharge's root key.
- ThirdPartyVerification: groups the shared verification context
  (context, discharges, unbound_signature, active_discharges) to
  reduce parameter count on the recursive path.

The public verify_with_discharges API is unchanged — it initializes
the active_discharges stack and delegates to the inner recursive method.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
